# meatsuit

> Make AI-generated text read like a person wrote it. A skill (Claude + Codex) that edits a
> draft to remove machine-writing tells, plus a zero-dependency detector that scores how "AI"
> text reads. An editor, not a generator. Never fabricates facts. Signals, not proof.

## What it does

Removes the statistical fingerprints of LLM writing — inflated vocabulary (delve, leverage,
robust), reframe constructions ("it's not X, it's Y"), forced rule-of-three, bullet points with
bold titles, even sentence rhythm, and assistant residue — while preserving meaning, register,
and the author's voice.

## Use it

- Claude skill: clone into ~/.claude/skills/meatsuit, then ask to "humanize this" / "remove AI tells".
- Claude/Cowork plugin: /plugin marketplace add good3n/meatsuit then /plugin install meatsuit.
- Codex / other agents: follow AGENTS.md.
- Standalone detector: `npx meatsuit <file>` (also --json, --context, stdin). Node >=18, no deps.

## Files

- SKILL.md — the Claude entry point (workflow, modes, output format, constraints).
- AGENTS.md — the Codex / general-agent entry point. Same rules.
- references/banned-vocabulary.md — era-grouped word lists, three severity tiers.
- references/banned-structures.md — reframe ban, rule-of-three, analogy control, formatting, weak verbs.
- references/rewrites.md — how to fix what the scan finds.
- references/examples.md — before/after calibration pairs.
- references/preserve.md — false-positive discipline and human signals to keep.
- detector/meatsuit.js — the scanner. detector/CATEGORIES.md — its issue types.

## Trigger phrases

humanize this, make it sound human, this reads like ChatGPT, remove the AI tells, de-AI this,
rewrite this robotic copy, edit for voice, make it less generic, strip the buzzwords.

MIT. Anyone may use, copy, modify, and ship it.
